title: Remote Access and Network Management Paths · Incident Case: Net Split Full Tunnel
summary: Provide secure operator, vendor, and user connectivity with recoverable in-band and out-of-band paths. Planned artifact: Add and verify the net split full tunnel increment in a least-privilege management access plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose split or full tunnel by traffic path, inspection, performance, privacy, DNS, and local-network risk.
Compare site-to-site and client-to-site VPNs, clientless access, authentication, authorization, and route scope.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Full tunnel guarantees all endpoint traffic is secure even when the device is compromised. This fails the Net Split Full Tunnel contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: Zero Trust Architecture (NIST SP 800-207) — Controls zero-trust terminology, policy decision, trust, identity, and least-privilege coverage.

steps.title: Build Net Split Full Tunnel evidence
